title: Public services associate professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate medical and health services in hospitals, clinics, managed care organizations, public health agencies, or similar organizations. Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Inspect and investigate sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensure conformance with Federal, State, and local regulations and ordinances. (Based on 76 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment

Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Advise clients or community groups on issues related to improving general health, such as diet, exercise, diagnostic screenings, sanitation, hygiene, self-care, and social or intellectual development, ensuring parental understanding of childhood immunizations.
Provide basic health services, administer immunizations, and conduct home visits for pregnant women, newborns, or other high-risk individuals to monitor their progress or assess their needs.
Distribute informational or educational documents to inform members of a targeted community and engage with community members at meetings or health fairs to understand community issues.

Advise management on matters related to the administration of contracts, employee discipline, grievance procedures, and ensure company compliance with federal and state laws, including reporting requirements.
Conduct research and draft rules, regulations, and contract proposals for collective bargaining, and negotiate agreements on behalf of employers or workers.
Monitor company or workforce adherence to labor agreements and evaluate compliance with contracts regarding wages, hours, or conditions of employment.
Represent the company's or labor's position during negotiations and prepare evidence for hearings, including testimonies and support for resolutions to disputes.

Select mediators or arbitrators for labor disputes, mediate discussions between parties to reconcile differences, and advise on collective bargaining strategies and risk levels associated with negotiations.
Research employee benefit and health and safety practices and recommend changes or modifications to existing policies, while administering employee insurance and pension plans.
Maintain updated client records and assist in preparing and maintaining personnel records and handbooks, including job descriptions and salary scales.
Prepare reports to summarize job analysis performances, organizational trends, and conduct evaluations or surveys to collect data on job-related information.
Advocate for individual or community health needs, assist families in applying for social services, and report incidences of abuse as required.
Collect information to compile vital statistics about the general health of community members, while developing formal plans or contracts to improve overall health.
Identify specific health care needs of individuals and high-risk groups, and refer community members to necessary health services.
Conduct training programs and develop curricula for community and organizational training related to health services and labor relations.
Engage in professional development activities and respond to customer complaints, taking necessary action to resolve issues.
Evaluate vendor products and performance and negotiate contracts accordingly; assist in preparing governmental reports related to labor and health services.

Establish objectives and evaluative or operational criteria for units managed, analyze employment-related data, prepare required reports, and review and evaluate applicant qualifications or eligibility for specified licensing according to established guidelines and designated licensing codes.

Contact job applicants to inform them of the status of their applications, conduct exit interviews and ensure that necessary employment termination paperwork is completed, and interview job applicants to obtain information on work history, training, education, or job skills.

Coordinate with outside staffing agencies to secure temporary employees based on departmental needs, develop or implement recruiting strategies to meet current or anticipated staffing needs, and direct or conduct recruitment, hiring, and training of personnel.

Evaluate recruitment or selection criteria to ensure conformance to professional, statutory, or testing standards, recommending revisions as needed and evaluate selection or testing techniques by conducting research and conferring with management or supervisory personnel.

Provide management with information or training related to interviewing, performance appraisals, counseling techniques, or documentation of performance issues, and inform job applicants of details such as duties and responsibilities, compensation, benefits, and promotion opportunities.

Maintain current knowledge of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and affirmative action guidelines and laws, maintain and update human resources documents, and maintain employment records related to events such as hiring, termination, leaves, and transfers.
Address employee relations issues such as harassment allegations, work complaints, or other employee concerns and consult with medical, business, and community groups to discuss service problems.

Conduct inspections to ensure compliance with standards, investigate and resolve personnel problems within the organization, and scrutinize and recommend changes in rules, policies, standards, and regulations.
Inspect facilities, supplies, vehicles, and equipment to ensure conformance to standards, and collect data regarding potential hazards from new equipment or products linked to green practices.
Conduct any required research or follow-up activities related to employee training programs, and develop or expand and implement medical programs or health services.
Inspect work of personnel performing maintenance or inspection duties to ensure adherence to standards and procedures.
Plan, implement, and administer programs and services in a health care or medical facility, and conduct worker studies to determine whether specific instances of disease or illness are job-related.
Meet with civic, educational, and community groups to develop community programs and events, and maintain communication between governing boards and department heads.
Maintain logs, prepare reports, and direct the preparation, handling, and maintenance of departmental records to ensure compliance.
Prepare activity reports to inform management of status and implementation plans regarding programs, services, and quality initiatives.

Train staff on proper procedures and practices, maintaining logs of daily activities, including areas visited or activities performed, and prepare or calibrate equipment used to collect or analyze samples.
Prepare or edit organizational publications, such as employee newsletters or stockholders' reports, for internal or external audiences.
Confer with other managers to identify trends, key group interests or concerns, or provide advice on business decisions.

Consult with advertising agencies or staff to arrange promotional campaigns in various media for products, organizations, or individuals, and develop public relations strategies to influence public opinion or promote ideas.
Coach client representatives in effective communication with the public or employees, and respond to media inquiries by designating appropriate spokespersons or information sources.

Establish and maintain cooperative relationships with representatives of community, consumer, employee, or public interest groups, contributing to public relations objectives through speeches, public appearances, and promotional events.
Prepare reports on investigations, inspections, and actions taken, as well as process and review environmental permits, licenses, or related materials.
Inspect cargo and handling devices, and observe loading of freight to ensure compliance with health and safety regulations, recommending remedial procedures for any violations found.
Educate the public about health issues or enforce health legislation to promote health and understanding of health protection procedures.
Conduct environmental assessments, site inspections and investigations to manage compliance with environmental regulations, and develop environmental restoration and management plans.
Collect, synthesize, analyze, and report environmental data, including pollution measurements and sustainability assessments, to inform stakeholders and improve environmental practices.
Conduct feasibility and cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects, and create models or simulations to assess ecological conditions and impacts of pollutants.
Identify environmental impacts, recommend mitigation strategies, and apply for permits required for the implementation of environmental projects to ensure compliance with regulations.
Supervise or train staff in environmental and healthcare settings, ensuring effective compliance with safety and health regulations.
Develop and distribute information materials for patients and medical staff, and maintain knowledge of community services and resources available to patients.
Communicate scientific or technical information effectively to public or organizational audiences through briefings, workshops, or public hearings.
Evaluate the effectiveness of industrial ecology programs and conduct analyses of environmental performance across various sectors.
Monitor the environmental impact of development activities, pollution, or land degradation and investigate sources of pollution to develop abatement methods.
Plan or conduct field research on topics related to environmental sustainability, development, or ecological studies, participating in research projects and conferences.
Collect and analyze patient information to customize tests, ensuring clear communication of testing procedures and results to patients and medical professionals.
Participate in care plan meetings to assess patient progress, coordinating with healthcare professionals to update care plans as necessary.
Coordinate communication between patients and medical staff, addressing inquiries or complaints to ensure satisfaction and resolve issues promptly.
Work as part of a team of healthcare professionals to manage patient care by assisting with medical procedures, related duties, and ensuring compliance with laws and regulations.
Conduct various ocular tests, including visual field tests, tonometry, and assess abnormalities of color vision, refractive condition, and depth perception.
Assist physicians in performing ophthalmic procedures and administer topical or oral medications as prescribed.
Evaluate patients' physical, emotional, and mental health conditions; observe vital signs, and report any concerns to medical staff.
Provide personal care and support to patients with disabilities, including aiding in daily living tasks and developing strategies to promote wellness and independence.
Facilitate communication and coordination among healthcare team members, patients, and families to ensure a comprehensive care approach.
Prepare, maintain, and review patient records and documentation to ensure accurate tracking of health status and treatment plans.
Administer and monitor diagnostic tests and procedures, ensuring patient comfort and safety, and provide explanations to patients to foster cooperation.
Conduct research and analysis on environmental or compliance issues; prepare reports and policy recommendations based on findings.

Develop requirements for the use of patient immobilization devices and positioning aides, such as molds or casts, as part of treatment plans to ensure accurate delivery of radiation and comfort of patient. Develop radiation treatment plans in consultation with members of the radiation oncology team. Design the arrangement of radiation fields to reduce exposure to critical patient structures, such as organs, using computers, manuals, and guides. Educate patients on ophthalmic medical procedures, conditions of the eye, and appropriate use of medications. Respond to complaints or questions on wetland jurisdiction, providing information or clarification. Monitor projects during or after construction to ensure projects conform to design specifications. Analyze results of investigations to determine measures needed to maintain or restore proper soil management. Conduct environmental audits or inspections or investigations of violations. Monitor the implementation and impact of guidelines for nondiscriminatory employment practices. Inspect government property, such as construction sites or public housing, to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements.

Teach, train, supervise, or use the assistance of students, respiratory therapy technicians, or assistants. Prepare, care for, and dispose of tissue specimens taken for laboratory analysis. Provide emergency care, such as artificial respiration, external cardiac massage, or assistance with cardiopulmonary resuscitation. Set up and operate devices, such as mechanical ventilators, therapeutic gas administration apparatus, environmental control systems, or aerosol generators, following specified parameters of treatment. Monitor patient's physiological responses to therapy, such as vital signs, arterial blood gases, or blood chemistry changes, and consult with physician if adverse reactions occur. Explain treatment procedures to patients to gain cooperation and allay fears. Prepare reports related to investigations of equal opportunity complaints. Provide advice on proper standards and regulations or the development of policies, strategies, or codes of practice for environmental management. Prepare or direct the preparation of additional information or responses as requested by regulatory agencies. Maintain supply of fluids, such as plasma, saline, blood, or glucose, for use during operations. Prepare charts or graphs from data samples, providing summary information on the environmental relevance of the data.

Perform annual gynecologic exams, including pap smears and breast exams. Prepare or maintain technical files as necessary to obtain and sustain product approval. Conduct tests, such as electrocardiograms (EKGs), stress testing, or lung capacity tests, to evaluate patients' cardiopulmonary functions. Relate blood analysis results to a physician. Maintain charts that contain patients' pertinent identification and therapy information. Make emergency visits to resolve equipment problems. Perform bronchopulmonary drainage and assist or instruct patients in performance of breathing exercises. Analyze credit data and financial statements to determine the degree of risk involved in extending credit or lending money. Analyze financial data, such as income growth, quality of management, and market share to determine expected profitability of loans. Manage field offices or involve staff in cooperative ventures.

Coordinate or implement technical, financial, or administrative assistance programs for local government units to ensure efficient program implementation or timely responses to requests for assistance. Review proposed wetland restoration easements or provide technical recommendations. Gather information from geographic information systems (GIS) databases or applications to formulate land use recommendations. Advise land users, such as farmers or ranchers, on plans, problems, or alternative conservation solutions. Develop or conduct environmental studies, such as plant material field trials or wildlife habitat impact studies. Review grant applications or make funding recommendations. Prepare responses to customer requests for information, such as product data, written regulatory affairs statements, surveys, or questionnaires. Identify compliance issues that require follow-up or investigation. Keep informed regarding pending industry changes, trends, or best practices. Provide assistance to internal or external auditors in compliance reviews. Review clinical protocols to ensure collection of data needed for regulatory submissions.

Develop methods to minimize the impact of production processes on the environment, based on the study and assessment of industrial production, environmental legislation, and physical, biological, and social environments. Write grants to obtain funding for restoration projects. Create and transfer reference images and localization markers for treatment delivery, using image-guided radiation therapy. Monitor effects of pollution or land degradation and recommend means of prevention or control. Examine, evaluate and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations governing contract compliance of licenses and permits, and perform other compliance and enforcement inspection and analysis activities not classified elsewhere. Conduct radiation oncology-related research, such as improving computer treatment planning systems or developing new treatment devices. Review or approve amendments to comprehensive local water plans or conservation district plans. Confirm that all firm and regulatory policies and procedures have been documented, implemented, and communicated. Participate in internal or external audits. Monitor environmental impacts of development activities.

Analyze, manipulate, or process large sets of data using statistical software. Develop environmental educational programs and curricula for schools. Visit establishments to verify that valid licenses or permits are displayed and that licensing standards are being upheld. Investigate alleged license or permit violations. Conduct surveys, studies, or investigations of various land uses to inform corrective action plans. Develop water conservation or harvest plans, using weather information systems, irrigation information management systems, or other sources of daily evapotranspiration (ET) data. Prepare or maintain technical files as necessary to obtain and sustain product approval. Teach and train new recruits on compliance requirements and policies. Participate in work teams to plan, develop, or implement programs or policies for improving environmental habitats, wetlands, or groundwater or soil resources.

Develop soil maps. Compute cost estimates of different conservation practices, based on needs of land users, maintenance requirements, or life expectancy of practices. Create graphs, charts, or other visualizations to convey the results of data analysis using specialized software. Perform additional inspections or follow-up investigations of facilities, operations, or processes as directed. Coordinate recall or market withdrawal activities as necessary.
Prepare data to calculate sewer service charges and capacity fees.
Provide pre-, ongoing, and post-inspection follow-up assistance to governmental inspectors.

Evaluate applications, records, or documents to gather information about eligibility or liability issues, and examine permits, licenses, applications, and records to ensure compliance with licensing requirements.

Advise licensees or other individuals or groups concerning licensing, permit, or passport regulations, and provide technical review of data or reports to be incorporated into regulatory submissions to assure scientific rigor, accuracy, and clarity of presentation.
Prepare reports of activities, evaluations, recommendations, or decisions, and prepare correspondence, reports of inspections or investigations, or recommendations for action.
Monitor or trace the location of goods, and verify the identity of patient or operative site.
Discuss with surgeon the nature of the surgical procedure, including operative consent, methods of operative exposure, diagnostic or laboratory data, or patient-advanced directives or other needs.
Confer with officials in various agencies to facilitate clearance of goods through customs and quarantine, and provide information concerning the circumstances of death to relatives of the deceased.

Interview and investigate applicants for public assistance to gather information pertinent to their applications, and interview benefits recipients at specified intervals to certify their eligibility for continuing benefits.
Determine the nature of code violations and actions to be taken, and issue written notices of violation, participating in enforcement hearings as necessary.
Present research findings to groups of people, and compile reports, charts, or graphs that describe and interpret findings of analyses.

Initiate procedures to grant, modify, deny, or terminate assistance, or refer applicants to other agencies for assistance, and compute and authorize amounts of assistance for programs, such as grants, monetary payments, and food stamps.
Collect, identify, evaluate, or preserve case evidence, and examine records, reports, or other documents to establish facts or detect discrepancies.
Conduct internet-based and library research.
Perform descriptive and multivariate statistical analyses of data, using computer software, and analyze clinical data using appropriate statistical tools.

Verify the accuracy and validity of data entered in databases, correcting any errors, and perform quality control audits to ensure accuracy, completeness, or proper usage of clinical systems and data.
Prepare tables, graphs, fact sheets, and written reports summarizing research results, and complete reports and forms required to finalize cases.
Develop and implement research quality control procedures, and create data management or error-checking procedures and user manuals.

Determine which sites and violation reports to investigate, and coordinate compliance and enforcement activities with other government agencies, including investigating complaints and suspected violations regarding illegal dumping, pollution, pesticides, product quality, or labeling laws.

Monitor follow-up actions in cases where violations were found, and prepare written, oral, tabular, and graphic reports summarizing requirements and regulations, including enforcement and chain of custody documentation.
Verify that hazardous chemicals are handled, stored, and disposed of in accordance with regulations, and evaluate label information for accuracy and conformance to regulatory requirements.
Inform individuals and groups of pollution control regulations and inspection findings, and explain how problems can be corrected.
Participate in the development of spill prevention programs and hazardous waste rules and regulations, and recommend corrective actions for hazardous waste problems.
Perform laboratory tests on samples collected, such as analyzing the content of contaminated wastewater, and collect specimens for use in laboratory tests.
Conduct ongoing prenatal health assessments, tracking changes in physical and emotional health, and develop, implement, or evaluate individualized plans for midwifery care.

Monitor maternal condition during labor by checking vital signs, and provide comfort and relaxation measures for mothers in labor through interventions such as massage, breathing techniques, or hydrotherapy.
Communicate with regulatory agencies regarding pre-submission strategies, potential regulatory pathways, compliance test requirements, or clarification and follow-up of submissions under review.
Research and keep informed of pertinent information and developments in areas such as EPA laws and regulations.
Counsel women regarding the nutritional requirements of pregnancy, and provide information about the physical and emotional processes involved in the pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum periods.
Inform health professionals, property owners, and the public about harmful properties and related problems of water pollution and contaminated wastewater.
Compile and evaluate clinical practice statistics, and prepare data analysis listings and activity, performance, or progress reports.

Evaluate patients' laboratory and medical records, requesting assistance from other practitioners when necessary, and obtain complete health and medical histories from patients including medical, surgical, reproductive, or mental health histories.
Collect and document any pertinent medical history information, and enhance knowledge through continuing education or professional associations to maintain awareness of current practices.
Analyze competitive market strategies through analysis of related product, market, or share trends, and identify and analyze industry or geographic trends with business strategy implications.
Analyze or manipulate bioinformatics data using software packages, statistical applications, or data mining techniques.
Train staff on technical procedures or software program usage, including supporting staff in database-related research activities.
Propose improvements to systems or processes in a manner that increases productivity and efficiency.
Conduct quality analyses of data inputs and resulting analyses or predictions.
Create business intelligence tools or systems, including design of related databases, spreadsheets, or outputs, and develop technical specifications for data management programming.

Collaborate with other professionals, such as lawyers, accountants, or public relations experts, and conduct financial analyses related to investments in green construction or green retrofitting projects.
Confer with clients to restructure, refinance, or raise new debt and advise them on aspects of capitalization such as amounts, sources, or timing.
Assess companies as investments for clients by examining facilities and analyze financial or operational performance of companies facing difficulties to identify or recommend remedies.
Prepare plans of action for investment and recommend investments and investment timing to companies or the public.
Supervise, train, or mentor junior team members and conduct in-service training sessions on relevant topics.

Read current literature, participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in relevant fields, and collect and compile data to document client performance or assess program quality.
Assist healthcare professionals in conducting assessments or treatments, such as preparing materials, maintaining supplies, and scheduling activities.
Document clients' progress toward meeting established treatment objectives and maintain accurate and complete records as required by laws and regulations.

Conduct follow-up interviews with clients to determine if their needs have been met and counsel individuals or groups to help them address problems affecting their educational or vocational situations.
Plan and conduct orientation programs and group conferences to assist individuals in adjusting to new experiences, and prepare students or individuals for future educational opportunities.
Collaborate with various stakeholders in developing, evaluating, and revising programs or policies related to public services, and participate in recruitment and enrollment activities.
Review files or transcripts to ensure compliance with graduation requirements and recommend qualified services to clients for their vocational needs.
Evaluate needs for assistance and provide information or referrals to appropriate services, ensuring compliance with regulations while drafting reports on progress or activities.
Conduct inspections using relevant instruments, engaging with stakeholders to explain regulations or recommend actions, and monitor activities for compliance with established standards.
Inspect and investigate practices related to public safety and health and recommend corrective actions or improvements when necessary.
Develop and maintain health record indexes or systems for data management and oversee the security of medical records to ensure confidentiality.
Inspect food or agricultural products for compliance with health and safety regulations and ensure quality standards are met.
Conduct environmental inspections to identify hazards in various settings and ensure adherence to regulatory requirements regarding safety and health.
Compile and analyze occupational data to assist clients in determining vocational objectives and prepare reports based on findings.

Inspect buildings and properties to ensure compliance with fire codes, safety regulations, and environmental impact, including identifying hazardous conditions, issuing permits, and recommending corrective actions to property owners or representatives.

Monitor the work of contractors in the design, construction, and startup phases of security systems, and inspect stations, uniforms, equipment, or recreation areas to ensure compliance with safety standards.

Outline system security criteria for pre-bid meetings with clients and companies to ensure comprehensiveness and appropriateness for implementation, and provide system design and integration recommendations.
Perform risk analyses to develop appropriate countermeasures and recommend improvements in security systems or procedures.
Prepare documentation for case reports or court proceedings, and write reports summarizing testing activities, including goals, planning, execution, results, and recommendations.
Prepare, maintain, or update security procedures, security system drawings, or related documentation, and create or administer training and awareness presentations or materials.
Respond to emergency situations on an on-call basis and provide emergency medical services as required.

Test security measures for final acceptance and implement ongoing monitoring and evaluation procedures for the measures, and review existing disaster recovery, crisis management, or business continuity plans.
Train personnel in security procedures or use of security equipment, and instruct fire department personnel in assigned duties, including firefighting and related subjects.
Interpret government regulations and applicable codes to ensure compliance and recommend or implement methods to monitor and enable resolution of safety, operations, or compliance interruptions.
Establish, maintain, or test call trees to ensure appropriate communication during disaster and develop emergency management plans for recovery decision making and continuity of operations.
Design or implement products and services to mitigate risk, and create business continuity and disaster recovery budgets.

Identify or investigate violations related to natural resources, waste management, recycling, or other environmental policies, and research regulatory, technical, or market issues related to sustainability.
Review and revise sustainability proposals or policies, and analyze corporate intelligence data to identify threats to security of people, assets, information, or infrastructure.

Analyze impact on, and risk to, essential business functions or information systems to identify acceptable recovery time periods and resource requirements, and prepare reports summarizing operational results or financial performance.
Dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to emergency reports and drive vehicles to complete work assignments.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems, and monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
